role_title: Senior Design Program Manager, Design Foundations & AI Transformation

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

secondary_archetype: Design Program Manager — Design Systems & AI Transformation

level_signals: 5+ years in design program management or relevant role
JD body calls role 'Staff Design Program Manager' while title says 'Senior Design Program Manager'
Comp range $118K–$147.5K base in NYC, consistent with senior/staff IC
Leads cross-functional AI Taskforce and owns roadmap end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in design program management, product design, or a relevant design industry role
Demonstrated ability to drive complex, cross-functional programs with multiple stakeholders and workstreams
Comfort with ambiguity — you can take a loosely defined problem, build a plan, and move without heavy oversight
Strong written and verbal communication; you know how to give direction clearly and tailor it to the audience
Hands-on familiarity with AI tools and a genuine interest in how they change the way design teams work
Attention to detail and organizational rigor; you don't let things fall through the cracks
Resilience — design orgs are dynamic, priorities shift, and this role requires someone who stays grounded

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with Figma, Jira, and GitHub a plus

scope.surface: Design Foundations & AI Transformation — Design Systems, Content Design, illustration, iconography, AI tooling, AI Taskforce, AI Education workstreams
primary_metrics_inferred: Roadmap delivery and on-time execution for Design Systems and AI projects
Team utilization and project health visibility
AI tool adoption across Design+ practitioners
core_responsibilities: Own roadmap and execution for Design Systems, illustration, iconography, and AI projects end-to-end
Drive program management for the AI Taskforce — coordinating across Education, Content Design, and Infrastructure Partnership tracks
Shape and document workflows and processes that make the team faster and more effective
Partner with designers, engineers, illustrators, and PMs to scope work, set timelines, and manage delivery
Build and maintain visibility into project health, team utilization, and priorities for design leadership
Lead planning and prioritization across cross-functional teams — balancing competing inputs without losing sight of the goal
Facilitate design reviews, retros, and planning sessions
Bring AI fluency to program management work — use tools like Figma, Jira, and AI assistants to accelerate your own workflow, and help the team adopt new tools effectively

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($118K–$147.5K base in NYC)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Design Foundations + AI Taskforce)
Specific days named for hybrid schedule (Mon/Wed/Thu), reducing ambiguity
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base
Generous parental leave (18 weeks) and strong benefits package
red_flags: JD body refers to role as 'Staff Design Program Manager' while the role title says 'Senior Design Program Manager' — potential level inconsistency worth clarifying with recruiter
Hybrid requires 3 days/week in-office (NYC) — no remote option
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, JD body 'Staff' label, and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.equity_form (mentioned as 'potential equity offering' but form not specified)
ambiguities: Role title in posting says 'Senior Design Program Manager' but the JD body opening paragraph says 'Lyft is seeking a Staff Design Program Manager' — the seniority level is inconsistent and should be confirmed with the recruiter.

Figma and Jira appear both in the core responsibilities section (implied required) and explicitly called out as 'a plus' in the Experience section — treated as nice-to-have per the JD's explicit 'a plus' language, but they are also referenced in responsibilities.
